title: Battlefield 1

notes:
Just found out as well. Literally no reason to do this, dedicated servers already have basement dwelling admins that ban cheaters the second they pull something off.

Before people complain that this is a premature report and I'm fearmongering for no reason, [BF5](https://www.protondb.com/app/1238810) and [BF2042](https://www.protondb.com/app/1517290) have the same anticheat and they're both unplayable on Linux. Nevermind that cheaters already figured out how to bypass it.

title: Battlefield 1

notes:
As off today, the 22nd October, this game is no longer playable due to EA being one of the worst gaming companies that exist. They now pushed a kernel level anticheat into an eight year old game which has the most dedicated basement dwelling moderator teams known to man. 
Other reports which state that the game is playable didnt bother looking up when the kernel level anti cheat is pushed into the game.

Here the link for all those that didnt bother looking it up: https://answers.ea.com/t5/Battlefield-1/EA-anticheat-is-coming-to-Battlefield-1-on-October-22nd/td-p/14005416

This is the faith of all battlefield games and since EA isnt going to listen to your email that they should "prewwy pwleease" enable proton/wine support they are going to stay borked for a long time. Its prolly going to be playable again when some guy is going to publish his 50 line dissable script that he wrote in 20 minutes to github.

If you are interested in a WW1 shoot, give Isonzo a try. Looks promissing, Indie devs and no anticheat which violates your freedom.
title: Battlefield 1
